sections.paragraph:
προσηνῶς (cp. Hom. antonym ἀπηνής ‘ungentle’; Theophr.; Diod S 5, 44, 6 et al.) adv. of προσηνής, ές (Pind., Hdt. et al.; SIG 783, 29; Pr 25:25; Philo; Jos., Bell. 3, 507; Mel., P. 11, 72) pert. to being kindly disposed, kindly, gently, lovingly w. ἠπίως of God (Orph. Hymns 2, 5; 40, 12; 60, 7 Qu. use the adj. to characterize goddesses) 1 Cl 23:1.—DELG s.v. προσηνής.

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Theophr Theophr , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

Orph. Orph. Hymns = Orphic Hymns, s. Orphica

adj. adj. = adjective

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 880
